windows-gfx1101.blocked_reason: torch::cuda::is_available() returns false in standalone C++ exe on Windows TheRock build: CUDAHooksRegistry stays empty because torch_hip.dll REGISTER_CUDA_HOOKS global constructors do not run via PE loader (lld-link omits .CRT$XCU table; _DllMainCRTStartup CRT init ptr is null; TLS callbacks fire only on thread-attach, not process-attach). Native HIP kernel tests 320/914 pass on real gfx1101 GPU (hipGetDeviceCount=1 confirmed); 564 tests fail because each guards on torch::cuda::is_available(); 30 are skipped for same reason. Torch parity oracle and tensor creation tests all require is_available()=true. Cannot fix without rebuilding torch_hip.dll with MSVC link.exe or explicitly registering CUDAHooks at exe startup.

windows-gfx1201.blocked_reason: Host-wide block, GPU-independent (carried from windows-gfx1101 determination): TheRock Windows torch_hip.dll (lld-link, no .CRT$XCU) leaves torch::cuda::is_available()==false in the standalone C++ test exe; native HIP kernels pass but torch-gated tests cannot run. Host toolchain bug, identical on gfx1201.

windows-gfx1151.blocked_reason: Recurring stall on windows-gfx1151 delta-port: TheRock/Windows clang-offload-bundler fails on the project's -fgpu-rdc link path (user-mapped-section / alien-blocker class, see windows-fgpu-rdc-bundler-bug memory); no viable Windows build after repeated attempts. Linux gfx90a+gfx1100 completed. Blocked pending a TheRock bundler fix or an --offload-new-driver path that LichtFeld's CMake accepts.
